what_it_does: Acceptance test for ops/agent_sessions_exporter.py that builds synthetic JSONL session fixtures in a temporary directory and runs the exporter multiple times to verify keep/strip/redact rules, title fallback, quiesce window, idempotence, read-only source, and the thinking-block toggle.
observations: Uses a tempfile-based fixture tree with a main session, a >2KB subagent, a <2KB stub, and a no-title session.
Defines a global PASS/FAIL counter and a check() helper that prints emoji pass/fail lines.
Ages all source files with os.utime so they pass the quiesce window, then later writes a fresh-mtime session to test quiesce skipping.
Asserts exact string presence/absence in generated Markdown to enforce the FILTER LAW contract.
Tests that the last ai-title occurrence wins, then falls back to last-prompt and first-user-turn.
Verifies subagent rollup is included while stub subagent is skipped, and that the subagent heading secret is redacted.
Runs the exporter four times: initial export, idempotence check, quiesce check, and --include-thinking toggle.
standouts: Encodes a policy-like FILTER LAW as executable assertions (KEEP/STRIP/REDACT/QUIESCE/IDEMPOTENT).
Explicitly tests a reviewed leak vector: secret redaction inside the subagent rollup heading.
Synthetic fixtures include realistic secret patterns (API keys, AWS keys, JWTs, assignment-style secrets).
Tests read-only source law by comparing pre/post source size and mtime snapshots.
conventions: Heavy docstring maps test coverage to a design guide section (Session Fabric F1.4, guide §E).
Uses sys.path.insert to import an ops module from a sibling directory.
Fixture builder returns the project path and session ids for downstream assertions.
Multiple exporter runs reuse the same source/dest tree to test cumulative behaviors.
Emoji and global counters give quick visual pass/fail summary.
concerns: Global mutable PASS/FAIL counters make parallel execution unsafe.
String-based assertions on exporter Markdown output are tightly coupled to the exporter's formatting.
Quiesce and idempotence checks rely on filesystem mtime granularity and time.time().
No explicit cleanup assertion for the live fixture written during the test.
The _line helper signature in the symbol list says _line(kw) but the code defines _line(**kw).
notes: A policy-driven acceptance harness that turns the session-export FILTER LAW into concrete, runnable checks over synthetic agent sessions.
questions: What is the exact quiesce window duration used by the exporter?
What redaction regexes or classifiers does the exporter apply to produce the [redacted:*] tokens?
How does the exporter decide a file is a subagent of a main session?
What is the precise Markdown template the exporter writes?
What does --force do, and how does it interact with quiesce/idempotence?
How are multiple subagents ordered or deduplicated in the rollup?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the implementation: the docstring lists keep/strip/redact/title/quiesce/idempotence/read-only/thinking behaviors, and the code asserts each of them.
intention_signals: To make the exporter's filtering policy executable and regression-proof by encoding it as law-named acceptance assertions on controlled synthetic data.
novelty: Frames acceptance testing as enforcement of a FILTER LAW with named legal-style properties (KEEP, STRIP, REDACT, QUIESCE, IDEMPOTENT).
contribution: A runnable behavioral specification that documents expected exporter semantics through concrete synthetic input/output examples and law-named assertions; it preserves the intended filtering contract for any fused system.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test that builds synthetic agent-session JSONL fixtures and asserts the exporter's keep/strip/redact/title/quiesce/idempotence/read-only/thinking-filter behaviors.
intention_for_embedding: To encode the session-export FILTER LAW as executable, regression-detecting checks against synthetic data.

symbols.does: Increments global PASS/FAIL and prints a pass/fail line for a named assertion.
symbols.description: The check helper is the assertion primitive of the test: it evaluates a boolean condition, updates global counters, and emits a ✅ or ❌ line with an optional detail string.

symbols.does: Serializes keyword arguments into one JSONL line for fixture construction.
symbols.description: The _line helper takes arbitrary keyword arguments, JSON-encodes them, and appends a newline so callers can concatenate synthetic exporter input lines.

symbols.does: Creates a temporary project directory with a rich main session, subagent, stub, and no-title session.
symbols.description: build_fixture constructs the synthetic source catalog used by the acceptance test, including noise lines, secrets, tool traces, title lines, a >2KB subagent, a <2KB stub, and a second session lacking titles.

symbols.does: Orchestrates the exporter runs and all FILTER LAW assertions.
symbols.description: main sets up the temp source and destination trees, ages files, runs the exporter four times under different configurations, and checks keep/strip/redact/title/quiesce/idempotence/read-only/thinking behaviors.
